#starting with the file uploads library(shiny) library(shinymeta) library(tidyverse) library(ggplot2) library(readr) library(lubridate) library(colourpicker) library(plotly) library(hexbin) library(bslib) library(shinycssloaders) library(shinyWidgets) # Increase upload limit to 50MB options(shiny.maxRequestSize = 50 * 1024^2) my_custom_theme <- bs_theme( version = 5, bootswatch = "lux", bg = "#ffffff", fg = "black", primary = "#0021A5", secondary = "#FA4616", base_font = font_google("Open Sans"), heading_font = font_google("Montserrat"), code_font = font_google("Fira Code") ) #ui ui <- fluidPage( #page theme theme = my_custom_theme, # App title titlePanel("Uploading Files"), # Sidebar layout with input and output definitions sidebarLayout( # Sidebar panel for inputs sidebarPanel( style = "background-color: #ddefff; border-color: #black;", #Input: Select a file fileInput("file1", "Choose CSV File", multiple = FALSE, accept = c("text/csv", "text/comma-separated-values,text/plain", ".csv")), tags$hr(), h3("Data Processing"), checkboxInput("remove_na", "Exclude Rows with Missing Data", value = TRUE), checkboxInput("log_scale", "Use Logarithmic Scale (Y-axis)", value = FALSE), shinyWidgets::switchInput( inputId = "trendline", label = "Trendline", value = FALSE, onLabel = "ON", offLabel = "OFF", onStatus = "primary" ), checkboxInput("use_hex", "Large Dataset Mode (Check if you have a large dataset!!)", value = TRUE), conditionalPanel( condition = "input.use_hex==true", sliderInput("hex_bins", "Hexbin Resolution", min = 10, max = 100, value = 30) ), # Horizontal line tags$hr(), # Input: Checkbox if file has header checkboxInput("header", "Header", TRUE), # Input: Select separator radioButtons("sep", "Separator", choices = c(Comma = ",", Semicolon = ";", Tab = "\t"), selected = ","), # Input: Select quotes radioButtons("quote", "Quote", choices = c(None = "", "Double Quote" = '"', "Single Quote" = "'"), selected = '"'), # Horizontal line tags$hr(), # Input: Select number of rows to display radioButtons("disp", "Display", choices = c(Head = "head", All = "all"), selected = "head"), selectInput("x_var", "Choose X-Axis", choices = NULL), selectInput("y_var", "Choose Y-Axis", choices = NULL), #global plot settings tags$hr(), h3("Global Plot Settings"), colourpicker::colourInput( "plot_color", "Plot Color", value = "#0021A5" ), sliderInput( "point_size", "Point Size", min = 1, max = 10, value = 3 ), sliderInput( "bins", "Histogram Bins", min = 5, max = 100, value = 30 ), selectInput( #themes "theme", "Theme", choices = c( "IFAS Theme", "Minimal", "Classic", "BW", "Light", "Dark" ) ), selectInput( #shape of dots in scatter "point_shape", "Point Shape", choices = c("Circle" = 16, "Star" = 8, "Square" = 15, "Diamond" = 18, "Triangle" = 17, "Cross" = 4), selected = 16 ), sliderInput("plot_alpha", "Opacity", min = 0, max = 1, value = 0.7), selectInput("color_var", "Color by (Optional):", choices = NULL), #second category color tags$hr(), uiOutput("na_warning") ), # Main panel for displaying outputs mainPanel( card( full_screen = TRUE, style = "border: 1px solid #e2e9f0; box-shadow: 0 4px 6px -1px rgba(0,0,0,0.5); margin-top:15px;", # Output: Data file #initial table tabsetPanel( type = "tabs", tabPanel("Data Preview", wellPanel( style = "background-color: #ddefff; border-left: 5px solid #0021A5; border-right: none; border-top: none; border-bottom: none; border-radius:4px; margin-bottom: 15px; margin-top: 10px;", h4("Data Structure Verification", style = "color: #0021A5; margin-top:0;font-weight: bold;"), p("View the raw data structure and column types below to ensure accuracy before running statistical modeling.", style = "margin-bottom: 0; color: #333333;") ), div(style = "margin-bottom: 15px;", downloadButton("downloadData", "Download CSV", class = "btn-secondary") ), tableOutput("contents") ), #summary stats tabPanel("Data Summary", wellPanel( style = "background-color: #ddefff; border-left: 5px solid #0021A5; border-right: none; border-bottom:none; border-radius: 4px; margin-bottom:15px; margin-top: 10px;", h4("Data Overview", style = "color: #0021A5; margin-top:0; font-weight:bold;"), p("This section shows summary statistics") ), wellPanel( style = "background-color: #ddefff; border-left: 5px solid #0021A5; border-right: none; border-bottom:none; border-radius:4px; margin-bottom:15px; margin-top: 10px;", h4("Data Summary and Model Estimates", style = "color:#0021A5; margin-top:0; font-weight:bold;"), p("Review the descriptive variables next to the standard ordinary least squares (OLS) baseline regression estimates. ", style = "margin-bottom:0; color: #333333;") ), fluidRow( column(width = 6, h5("Descriptive Statistics", style = "color: #0021A5; font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 15px;"), div(style = "overflow-x: auto;",tableOutput("summaryStats")) ), column(width = 6, h5("Baseline OLS Regression Coefficients", style = "color: #0021A5; font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 15px;"), div(style = "overflow-x: auto;", tableOutput("regressionTable")) ) ), ), #scatterplot tabPanel(" Interactive Scatter Plot", wellPanel( style = "background-color: #ddefff; border-left: 5px solid #0021A5; border-right: none; border-bottom:none; border-radius: 4px; margin-bottom:15px; margin-top: 10px", h4("Best for: Showing Relationships and Correlations", style = "color:#0021A5; margin-top:0; font-weight:bold;"), p("A scatterplot works best for comparing two numeric variables. It helps identify if one variable is correlated to the other and reveals outliers and clusters in datasets. If you have a large dataset, please use the hexbin to avoid crashing from plotting so many individual interactive points. Uncheck large dataset mode to return to scatterplot. See next tab for a static scatterplot.", style = "margin-bottom: 0; color: #333333;") ), downloadButton("downloadScatter", "Download Plot as PNG"), shinycssloaders::withSpinner(plotlyOutput("myPlot", height = "600px"), type = 4, color = "#FA4616"), actionButton("showCodePlot", "Show Code for Scatter Plot" ),#code button verbatimTextOutput("plotCodeDisplay")), #Static Scatterplot for large data sets tabPanel("Static Scatter Plot", wellPanel( style = "background-color: #ddefff; border-left: 5px solid #0021A5; border-right: none; border-bottom:none; border-radius: 4px; margin-bottom:15px; margin-top: 10px", h4("Best for: Large Data Sets", style = "color:#0021A5; margin-top:0; font-weight:bold;"), p("This plot displays a static scatter plot."), ), downloadButton("downloadStaticScatter", "Download Static Plot as a PNG"), plotOutput("myStaticPlot", height = "600px"), actionButton("showCodeStaticPlot", "Show Code for Static Scatterplot"), verbatimTextOutput("staticPlotCodeDisplay") ), #histogram tabPanel("Histogram", wellPanel( style = "background-color: #ddefff; border-left: 5px solid #0021A5; border-right: none; border-bottom:none; border-radius: 4px; margin-bottom:15px; margin-top:10px", h4("Best for: Distribution and Frequency", style = "color:#0021A5; margin-top:0; font-weight:bold;"), p("Histograms are Best for: looking at a single numeric variable. They show if the data is normally distributed(bell curve), skewed to one side, or has multiple peaks") ), selectInput("hist_var", "Select Numeric Column", choices = NULL), downloadButton("downloadHist", "Download PNG"), actionButton("showCodeHist", "Show Code for Histogram"), plotlyOutput("myHist"), verbatimTextOutput("histCodeDisplay")), tabPanel("Box Plot", wellPanel( style = "background-color: #ddefff; border-left: 5px solid #0021A5; border-right: none; border-bottom:none; border-radius: 4px; margin-bottom:15px; margin-top:10px", h4("Best for: Multiple Categories of Data", style = "color:#0021A5; margin-top:0; font-weight:bold;"), p("Boxplots are best for visualizing the spread and outliers of numeric varaibles across different categories") ), downloadButton("downloadBox", "Download Plot as PNG"), plotlyOutput("myBox"), actionButton("showCodeBox", "show code for box plot"), verbatimTextOutput("boxCodeDisplay")), #time trend tabPanel("Time Trend", wellPanel( style = "background-color: #ddefff; border-left: 5px solid #0021A5; border-right: none; border-bottom:none; border-radius: 4px; margin-bottom:15px; margin-top:10px", h4("Best for: Patterns over Time", style = "color:#0021A5; margin-top:0; font-weight: bold;"), p("Time Trends work best with a date/time variable. They help visualize seasonality, long-term growth, or sudden drops across a chronological sequence.") ), downloadButton("downloadTrend", "Download"), plotlyOutput("timeTrend"), actionButton("showCodeTrend", "Show Code for Time Trend"), verbatimTextOutput("trendCodeDisplay")) ) ) ) )) ifas_blue <- "#0021A5" ifas_orange <- "#FA4616" theme_ifas <- function() { theme_minimal(base_size = 12) %+replace% theme( plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, color = ifas_orange, hjust = 0, margin = margin(b = 10)), axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = ifas_orange), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#e7e7e7", linewidth = .5), legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"), plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA), panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA) ) } apply_theme <- function(p, theme_choice){ switch(theme_choice, "IFAS Theme" = p + theme_ifas(), "Minimal" = p + theme_minimal(), "Classic" = p + theme_classic(), "BW" = p + theme_bw(), "Light" = p + theme_light(), "Dark" = p + theme_dark(), p + theme_minimal() #default ) } # Define server logic to read selected file server <- function(input, output, session) { cleanedData <- reactive({ df <-userData() req(df) if(input$remove_na){ current_vars <- c(input$x_var, input$y_var, input$hist_var) valid_vars <- intersect(current_vars, names(df)) if(length(valid_vars) >0){ df <- df%>% filter(if_all(all_of(valid_vars), ~ !is.na(.))) } } df }) # 1. Data Loading userData <- metaReactive({ req(input$file1) read.csv(..(input$file1$datapath), header = ..(input$header), sep = ..(input$sep), quote = ..(input$quote)) }) # Update selectors when data is uploaded observeEvent(userData(), { df <- userData() cols <- names(df) nums <- names(df)[sapply(df, is.numeric)] updateSelectInput(session, "x_var", choices = cols, selected = cols[1]) updateSelectInput(session, "y_var", choices = cols, selected = if(length(cols) > 1) cols[2] else cols[1]) updateSelectInput(session, "color_var", choices = c("None", cols), selected = "None") updateSelectInput(session, "hist_var", choices = nums) }) #summary stats output$summaryStats <- renderTable({ req(userData()) df <- cleanedData() req(input$x_var) validate(need(is.numeric(df[[input$x_var]]), "Select a numeric column for summary stats.")) s <- summary(df[[input$x_var]]) stats_df <- data.frame( Statistic = names(s), Value = as.character(unname(s)) ) return(stats_df) }, striped = TRUE, hover = TRUE, width = "100%", digits = 4) #regression table output$regressionTable <- renderTable({ req(userData()) df <- cleanedData() req(input$x_var, input$y_var) validate( need(is.numeric(df[[input$x_var]]), "X-Axis must be a numeric column to peform a linear regression."), need(is.numeric(df[[input$y_var]]), "Y-Axis must be a numeric column to perform a linear regression."), ) formula_obj <- as.formula(paste0("`", input$y_var, "`~`", input$x_var, "`")) fit <- lm(formula_obj, data = df) fit_summary <- summary(fit)$coefficients coef_output <- data.frame( Term = rownames(fit_summary), Estimate = fit_summary[, "Estimate"], `Std. Error` = fit_summary[, "Std. Error"], `t-value` = fit_summary[, "t value"], `p-value` = fit_summary[, "Pr(>|t|)"], check.names = FALSE ) coef_output$Term <- ifelse( coef_output$Term == "(Intercept)", "Intercept (a)", paste0("Slope (β) of ", input$x_var) ) return(coef_output) }, digits = 4, striped = TRUE, hover = TRUE, width = "100%") output$na_warning <- renderUI({ req(userData(), cleanedData()) orig_count <- nrow(userData()) clean_count <- nrow(cleanedData()) removed <- orig_count - clean_count if(input$remove_na && removed >0){ wellPanel( style = "background-color: #fff3cd; border-color: #ffeeba; color: #856404;", tags$b("Data Health Note: "), paste0(removed, " rows containing missing values were removed from this dataset") ) } else{ return(NULL) } }) # creating the scatterplot scatter_obj <- metaReactive({ df <- cleanedData() req(df, input$x_var, input$y_var) validate( need(is.numeric(df[[input$x_var]]) || inherits(df[[input$x_var]], "Date"), "Please select a Numeric or Date column for the X Axis."), need(is.numeric(df[[input$y_var]]), "Please select a Numeric Column for the Y-Axis.") ) #if not using hexbin, max of 5000 data points if(!input$use_hex && nrow(df)> 5000){ set.seed(123) df <- df[sample(nrow(df), 5000),] } #date parsing to stop crashing if(is.character(df[[input$x_var]])){ test_val <- head(df[[input$x_var]], 20) parsed_test <- suppressWarnings( lubridate:: parse_date_time(test_val, orders = c("ymd", "mdy", "dmy"), quiet = TRUE) ) if(any(!is.na(parsed_test))){ df[[input$x_var]] <- as.Date(suppressWarnings( lubridate::parse_date_time(df[[input$x_var]], orders = c("ymd", "mdy", "dmy"), quiet = TRUE) )) } } num_colors <- if(!is.null(input$color_var) && input$color_var != "None") length(unique(df[[input$color_var]])) else 0 p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[input$x_var]], y = .data[[input$y_var]])) #hex if(input$use_hex) { p <- p + geom_hex(bins = ..(input$hex_bins)) + scale_fill_viridis_c() } else { if(input$color_var == "None" || num_colors >30){ p <- p + geom_point(size = ..(input$point_size), color = ..(input$plot_color), shape = ..(as.numeric(input$point_shape)), alpha = ..(input$plot_alpha)) } else{ p <- p + geom_point(aes(color = .data[[input$color_var]]), size = ..(input$point_size), shape = ..(as.numeric(input$point_shape)), alpha = ..(input$plot_alpha)) if(length(unique(df[[input$color_var]])) >30){ p <- p + theme(legend.position = "none") } } } #assigning colors show_color_legend <- input$color_var != "None" && !input$use_hex && length(unique(df[[input$color_var]])) <= 30 color_label <- if(show_color_legend) input$color_var else NULL p <- p + labs(title = paste("Scatterplot of", input$x_var, "vs", input$y_var), x = input$x_var, y = input$y_var, color = color_label) if(input$trendline) p <- p + geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "lm", color = "blue") apply_theme(p, input$theme) }) #output scatterplot output$myPlot <- renderPlotly({p <- scatter_obj() ggplotly(p) %>% layout(hovermode = "closest")}) #download scatterplot output$downloadScatter <- downloadHandler( filename = function() { paste0("scatter-", Sys.Date(), ".png") }, content = function(file) { ggsave(file, plot = scatter_obj(), device = "png", width = 8, height = 6) } ) #creating the scatterplot that is static for large dataset static_scatter_obj <- metaReactive({ df <- cleanedData() req(df, input$x_var, input$y_var) validate( need(is.numeric(df[[input$x_var]]) || inherits(df[[input$x_var]], "Date"), "Please select a Numeric or Date Column for the X-Axis."), need(is.numeric(df[[input$y_var]]), "Please select a Numeric Column for the Y-Axis.") ) #data parsing if(is.character(df[[input$x_var]])){ test_val <- head(df[[input$x_var]], 20) parsed_test <- suppressWarnings( lubridate:: parse_date_time(test_val, orders = c("ymd", "mdy", "dmy"),quiet = TRUE) ) if(any(!is.na(parsed_test))){ df[[input$x_var]] <- as.Date(suppressWarnings( lubridate:: parse_date_time(df[[input$x_var]], orders = c("ymd", "mdy", "dmy"), quiet = TRUE) )) } } num_colors <- if(!is.null(input$color_var) && input$color_var != "None") length(unique(df[[input$color_var]])) else 0 p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[input$x_var]], y = .data[[input$y_var]])) if(input$color_var=="None" || num_colors >30){ p <- p + geom_point(size = ..(input$point_size), color = ..(input$plot_color), shape = ..(as.numeric(input$point_shape)), alpha = ..(input$plot_alpha)) } else { p <- p + geom_point(aes(color = .data[[input$color_var]]), size = input$point_size, shape = as.numeric(input$point_shape), alpha = input$plot_alpha) } show_color_legend <- input$color_var != "None" && num_colors <=30 color_label <- if(show_color_legend) input$color_var else NULL p <- p + labs(title = paste("Static Scatterplot of", input$x_var, "vs", input$y_var), x = input$x_var, y = input$y_var, color = color_label) if(input$trendline) p <- p + geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "lm", color = "blue") apply_theme(p, input$theme) }) #output static scatterplot output$myStaticPlot <- renderPlot({ static_scatter_obj() }) #download static scatter output$downloadStaticScatter <- downloadHandler( filename = function(){ paste0("static-scatter-", Sys.Date(), ".png")}, content = function(file){ggsave(file, plot = static_scatter_obj(), device = "png", width = 8, height = 6)} ) # creating the histogram hist_obj <- metaReactive({ df <- cleanedData() req(df, input$hist_var) req(input$hist_var %in% names(df)) validate( need(is.numeric(df[[input$hist_var]]), "The selected column is not numeric. Please select a numeric variable to continue.") ) p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[input$hist_var]])) + geom_histogram(fill = ..(input$plot_color), color = "white", bins = ..(input$bins)) + labs(title = paste("Distribution of", input$x_var)) apply_theme(p, input$theme) }) #output the histogram output$myHist <- renderPlotly({ p <- hist_obj() ggplotly(p) }) #download histogram output$downloadHist <- downloadHandler( filename = function() { paste0("histogram-", Sys.Date(), ".png") }, content = function(file) { ggsave(file, plot = hist_obj(), device = "png") } ) #creating the boxplot box_obj <- metaReactive({ df <- cleanedData() req(df, input$x_var, input$y_var) validate(need(is.numeric(df[[input$y_var]]), "Boxplot requires a Numeric Column for the Y-Axis values") ) # Calculate how many unique categories are in the X variable num_categories <- length(unique(df[[input$x_var]])) # pls stop crashing validate( need(num_categories <= 50, paste("Too many categories (", num_categories, ") to display in a boxplot. Please select a column with fewer unique groups.")) ) p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = as.factor(.data[[input$x_var]]), y = .data[[input$y_var]])) + geom_boxplot(fill = ..(input$plot_color), alpha = ..(input$plot_alpha), outlier.shape = NA) + labs(title = paste("Boxplot of", input$y_var, "by", input$x_var), x = input$x_var, y = input$y_var) apply_theme(p, input$theme) }) #output the boxplot output$myBox <- renderPlotly({ p <- box_obj() ggplotly(p) }) #download boxplot output$downloadBox <- downloadHandler( filename = function() { paste0("boxplot-", Sys.Date(), ".png")}, content = function(file) {ggsave(file, plot = box_obj(), device = "png")} ) # creating the time trend processedData <- metaReactive({ df <- cleanedData() req(df) # 1. Identify potential date columns strictly is_date_col <- sapply(df, function(x) { # Skip if it's already a date, numeric, or logical if (inherits(x, "Date") || inherits(x, "POSIXt")) return(TRUE) if (is.numeric(x) || is.logical(x) || all(is.na(x))) return(FALSE) # Try parsing a small sample silently sample_val <- as.character(head(x[!is.na(x)], 20)) if (length(sample_val) == 0) return(FALSE) # Use suppressWarnings to stop the "All formats failed" message from crashing the log test_parse <- suppressWarnings( lubridate::parse_date_time(sample_val, orders = c("ymd", "mdy", "dmy"), quiet = TRUE) ) # If we successfully parsed more than 0 rows, it's a date candidate sum(!is.na(test_parse)) > 0 }) date_col <- names(df)[is_date_col][1] # 2. Stop if no date column is found validate( need(!is.na(date_col), "Could not find a Date column. Please ensure your data has a column like '2023-01-01' or '01/01/2023'.") ) # 3. Convert only the detected column df[[date_col]] <- as.Date(suppressWarnings( lubridate::parse_date_time(df[[date_col]], orders = c("ymd", "mdy", "dmy"), quiet = TRUE) )) df }) trend_obj <- metaReactive({ df <- processedData() d_col <- names(df)[sapply(df, function(x) inherits(x, "Date"))][1] n_col <- input$y_var # Using the user-selected Y variable req(d_col, n_col) validate( need(inherits(df[[d_col]], "Date") || inherits(df[[df_col]], "POSIXt"), "No valid Date Column detected in the dataset. Time trend cannot be drawn without this."), need(is.numeric(df[[n_col]]), "Please select a Numeric column for the Y-Axis to see its trend over time.") ) p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[d_col]], y = .data[[n_col]])) + geom_line(color = ..(input$plot_color), size = ..(input$point_size), alpha = ..(input$plot_alpha)) + labs(title = "Time Trend Analysis", x = d_col, y = n_col) if(input$trendline) p <- p + geom_smooth(method = "lm", linetype = "dashed") apply_theme(p, input$theme) }) #output time trend output$timeTrend <- renderPlotly({ p <- trend_obj() ggplotly(p) }) #download time trend output$downloadTrend <- downloadHandler( filename = function() { paste0("trend-", Sys.Date(), ".png") }, content = function(file) { ggsave(file, plot = trend_obj(), device = "png") } ) # creating table output$contents <- renderTable({ df <- userData() req(df) if(input$disp == "head") { return(head(df)) } else { # Never try to render more than 100 rows in a standard table # Standard render Table is not built for all in large data return(head(df, 100)) } }) #download table output$downloadData <- downloadHandler( filename = function() { paste0("data-", Sys.Date(), ".csv") }, content = function(file) { write.csv(userData(), file, row.names = FALSE) } ) # table code buttons observeEvent(input$showCodePlot, { output$plotCodeDisplay <- renderPrint({ expandChain(quote(library(ggplot2)), scatter_obj()) }) }) observeEvent(input$showCodeStaticPlot, { output$staticPlotCodeDisplay <- renderPrint({ expandChain(quote(library(ggplot2)), static_scatter_obj()) }) }) observeEvent(input$showCodeHist, { output$histCodeDisplay <- renderPrint({ expandChain(quote(library(ggplot2)), hist_obj()) }) }) observeEvent(input$showCodeBox, { output$boxCodeDisplay <- renderPrint({ expandChain(quote(library(ggplot2)), box_obj())}) }) observeEvent(input$showCodeTrend, { output$trendCodeDisplay <- renderPrint({ expandChain(quote(library(ggplot2)), trend_obj()) }) }) } # Create Shiny app shinyApp(ui, server)
